div1.entryFree: Insconce, to shelter, to hide: he entertained a show so seeming just, and therein so --d his secret evil, Lucr. 1515. against that time do I i. me here within the knowledge of mine own desert, Sonn. 49, 9. you will i. your rags under the shelter of your honour, Wiv. II, 2, 27. I will i. me behind the arras, III, 3, 96. I must get a sconce for my head and i. it too, Err. II, 2, 38. we make trifles of terrors, --ing ourselves into seeming knowledge, Alls II, 3, 4.
